channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Thermal detection of single photons using Dirac fermions
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 12 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70648-0

Single photon detectors are essential for various quantum and imaging applications. Here, the authors report graphene bolometers able to detect single near-infrared photons at temperatures up to 1.2 K with intrinsic quantum efficiency up to 87%, dark count < 1 per second and effective noise equivalent power down to 2 × 10−22 W/$$\sqrt{{{{\rm{Hz}}}}}$$ Hz.
item.dc:title: Thermal detection of single photons using Dirac fermions
item.dc:creator: Bevin Huang
Ethan G. Arnault
Woochan Jung
Caleb Fried
B. Jordan Russell
Kenji Watanabe
Takashi Taniguchi
Erik A. Henriksen
Dirk Englund
Gil-Ho Lee
Kin Chung Fong

item.title: Twist-induced orbital chirality in a photonic laser
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 12 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70408-0

The combination of two achiral photonic membranes into a twisted bilayer breaks mirror symmetry and induces non-Hermitian coupling between counter-rotating collective modes, making laser emission chiral.
item.dc:title: Twist-induced orbital chirality in a photonic laser
item.dc:creator: Kristina Frizyuk
Costantino De Angelis
